description:
GOAL: Produce a flow-insensitive Var -> Descr map for every FnIr in a Module, walking fz_ir directly (not the AST). No consumers yet — this ticket builds the typer; later tickets plug it into ir_codegen and ir_lower.

## Why this is atomic

After this ticket, every compile produces a typer output that is consumed by no one. Tests assert exact Descrs for representative programs. The repo stays green; existing 337 tests still pass; new typer tests exercise the new module.

## Deliverables

New file src/ir_typer.rs:
- `pub fn type_module(m: &Module) -> ModuleTypes` where `ModuleTypes = Vec<HashMap<Var, Descr>>` (indexed by fn position in m.fns).
- Walk each FnIr's blocks in any order (flow-insensitive in this ticket; flow-sensitive narrowing is .11.24.3).
- Per-Stmt typing rules:
  * `Const(Int n)` -> `Descr::int_lit(n)`
  * `Const(Float f)` -> `Descr::float_lit(f)` (uses F64Bits)
  * `Const(True/False)` -> `Descr::atom_top` for booleans? Use existing types.rs convention; document.
  * `Const(Atom id)` -> singleton atom Descr.
  * `Const(Nil)` -> nil Descr.
  * `BinOp(arith, a, b)` -> Int if both vars Descr <= int_top, else widen to numeric_top (Int or Float). Conservative for now.
  * `BinOp(Eq/Neq/cmp, _, _)` -> bool Descr.
  * `UnOp(Neg, v)` -> same as v's numeric Descr.
  * `MakeList(els, tail)` -> ListSig over union of element Descrs.
  * `MakeTuple(els)` -> TupleSig with element Descrs.
  * `MakeMap(entries)` -> MapSig from key/value Descrs.
  * `MakeBitstring(_)` -> bitstring Descr.
  * `MakeVec(kind, _)` -> vec Descr per kind.
  * `MakeClosure(fn_id, _)` -> ArrowSig for the target fn (from its FnIr signature in the module).
  * `ListCons / ListHead / ListTail / ListIsNil / TupleField / MapGet / BitReader*` -> conservative Top initially; .11.24.3 narrows these.
  * `AllocStruct / Builtin` -> Top.
- Term-level: `Term::Goto(target, args)` propagates arg Descrs into target block params (union over predecessors).
- `Term::Call(fn_id, args, cont)`: cont's first param is Top in this ticket; .11.24.7 will tighten via specialize_return.
- Block-param join: union of arg Descrs from all predecessors, computed iteratively to fixpoint.
- Pipeline hook: ir_codegen::compile() calls `type_module` after liveness analysis and before codegen. Result attached to CompiledModule for future consumers (or stored as Module field — pick during impl).

## Tests in src/ir_typer.rs

- A program with `fn main(), do: 42` types main's return Var as int_lit(42).
- A program with `fn add1(n), do: n + 1` types n + 1 as Int (param Var widens to int_top through call observations not yet present, so for now the body's Var is int_top).
- `fn f(), do: [1, 2, 3]` types the result as ListSig{ elem: int_top }.
- `fn f(), do: {1, :ok}` types the result as TupleSig{ elems: [int_lit(1), atom_lit('ok')] }.
- `fn f(), do: %{a: 1}` types as MapSig.
- A two-block fn with Term::Goto joining param shapes asserts the join is the union.

## Out of scope

- Pattern narrowing (.11.24.3).
- Any codegen / lower-time consumer (.11.24.4 / .11.24.5).
- Specialization-aware call return (.11.24.7).
- Flow-sensitive refinement on If branches (.11.24.3).

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY: fz-ul4.11.24.1.
- BLOCKS: fz-ul4.11.24.3.
title: IR-shaped inference driver: Var -> Descr per FnIr
